title: Advanced Interactivity
code: library(uwot) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) library(ggiraph)   # Prepare Data & Colour Schemes penguins <- na.omit(read.csv(system.file("penguins.csv", package = "ggEDA"))) palette_species <- c(Chinstrap = "#C55BCC", Adelie = "#FF7F02", Gentoo = "#047476")  # Create ggstack plot ggstack_penguins <- ggstack(   penguins,   col_id = "name",   interactive = FALSE,   col_sort = "species",   palettes = list(species = palette_species),   options = ggstack_options(relative_height_numeric = 1.2, show_legend = FALSE) )  # Perform the umap umap_mx <- umap(penguins, scale = "scale", n_neighbors = 70) df_umap <- as.data.frame(umap_mx) colnames(df_umap) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2") df_umap[["name"]] <- penguins[["name"]] df_umap[["species"]] <- penguins[["species"]]  # Create the umap plot gg_umap <- ggplot(df_umap, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2, colour = species, data_id = name)) +   geom_point_interactive() +   scale_color_manual(values = palette_species) +   theme_bw()  # Combine the ggstack plot and the umap using patchwork combined_plots <- free(gg_umap, type = "label") / free(ggstack_penguins, side = "tb")  # Make interactive interactive_multiplot <- girafe(ggobj = combined_plots, height_svg = 6, width_svg = 10, options)  # Add some settings to choose how to make combined plots interactive interactive_multiplot <- girafe_options(   x = interactive_multiplot,   opts_selection(type = "multiple", only_shiny = FALSE, css = "opacity: 1"),   opts_selection_inv(css = "opacity: 0.12") )  # Draw Interactive Multiplot interactive_multiplot

title: Gallery
text: simulated “Lazy Birdwatcher” dataset records magpie observations Robert Catherine. Robert doesn’t birdwatch weekends, resulting missing data days. pattern missingness, dependent person day, hard detect using one-dimensional EDA tools like skimr two-dimensional tools like ggpairs GGally package, don’t capture multi-variable interactions contributing missingness.
code: # Plot lazy birdwatcher dataset by Number of Magpies Observed ggstack(   lazy_birdwatcher,   col_sort = "Magpies",   palettes = list(     Birdwatcher = c(Robert = "#E69F00", Catherine = "#999999"),     Day = c(Weekday = "#999999", Weekend = "#009E73")   ),   options = ggstack_options(     show_legend = TRUE,     fontsize_barplot_y_numbers = 12,     legend_text_size = 16,     legend_key_size = 1,     legend_nrow = 1,   ) )

title: Parallel Coordinate Plots: Automaticaly Ordering Axes
text: Nearest Neighbour Tour (nn) path ‘next’ dimension always chosen based one lowest # crossings current current dimension Repetitive Nearest Neighbour Algorithm (rnn) Build separate nearest neighbour tour using dimension starting point. Return tour producing lowest number crossings Obviously certain configurations networks nearest neighbour algorithms find highly non-optimal paths. (partially) mitigate take best rnn solution improve using two-opt (.k.pairwise exchange) algorithm. Two-Opt systematically removes replaces two edges graph. run complete 2-opt local search, compare every possible valid combination swapping mechanism.

title: Parallel Coordinate Plots: Automaticaly Ordering Axes
text: can use algorithmic solution minimise metrics number crossings, including ‘approximations’ crossing numbers based subsets data (save time), mutual information distance pair dimensions (based Joe et al. 1989).

code: ggstack(   data,   col_id = NULL,   col_sort = NULL,   order_matches_sort = TRUE,   maxlevels = 7,   verbose = 2,   drop_unused_id_levels = FALSE,   interactive = TRUE,   return = c("plot", "column_info", "data"),   palettes = NULL,   sort_type = c("frequency", "alphabetical"),   desc = TRUE,   limit_plots = TRUE,   max_plottable_cols = 10,   cols_to_plot = NULL,   tooltip_column_suffix = "_tooltip",   ignore_column_regex = "_ignore$",   convert_binary_numeric_to_factor = TRUE,   options = ggstack_options(show_legend = !interactive) )

title: AutoPlot an entire data.frame — ggstack
text: data data.frame autoplot (data.frame) col_id name column use identifier. null, artificial IDs created based row-number. col_sort name columns sort . hierarchical sort, supply vector column names order sorted (character). order_matches_sort column plots stacked top--bottom order appear col_sort (flag) maxlevels categorical variables, maximum number distinct values allow (many make hard find palette suits). (number) verbose Numeric value indicating verbosity level: 2: Highly verbose, messages. 1: Key messages . 0: Silent, messages. drop_unused_id_levels col_id factor unused levels, dropped included visualisation interactive produce interactive ggiraph visualiastion (flag) return string describing function return. Options include: plot: Return ggEDA visualisation (default) colum_info: Return data.frame describing columns dataset. data: Return processed dataset used plotting. palettes list named vectors. List names correspond data column names (categorical ). Vector names levels columns. Vector values colours, vector names used map values data colour. sort_type controls categorical variables sorted. Numerical variables always sorted numerical order irrespective value given . Options alphabetical frequency desc sort descending order (flag) limit_plots throw error > max_plottable_cols dataset (flag) max_plottable_cols maximum number columns can plotted (default: 10) (number) cols_to_plot names columns data plotted. default plots valid columns (character) tooltip_column_suffix suffix added column name indicates column used tooltip (string) ignore_column_regex regex string , matches column name,  cause  column excluded plotting (string). NULL regex check performed. (default: "_ignore$") convert_binary_numeric_to_factor numeric column conatins values 0, 1, & NA, automatically convert factor. options list additional visual parameters created calling ggstack_options(). See ggstack_options details.

title: Visual Parameters for ggstack Plots — ggstack_options
text: colours_default Default colors categorical variables without custom palette. colours_default_logical Colors binary variables: vector three colors representing TRUE, FALSE, NA respectively (character). colours_missing Color missing (NA) values categorical plots (string). show_legend_titles Display titles legends (flag). legend_title_position Position legend title ("top", "bottom", "left", "right"). legend_nrow Number rows legend (number). legend_ncol Number columns legend. set, legend_nrow NULL (number). legend_title_size Size legend title text (number). legend_text_size Size text within legend (number). legend_key_size Size legend key symbols (number). legend_orientation_heatmap legend orientation "horizontal" "vertical". show_legend Display legend plot (flag). legend_position Position legend ("right", "left", "bottom", "top"). na_marker Text used mark NA values numeric plots (string). na_marker_size Size text marker NA values (number). na_marker_colour Color NA text marker (string). show_na_marker_categorical Show marker NA Infinite values categorical tiles (flag). show_na_marker_heatmap Show marker NA values heatmap tiles (flag). colours_heatmap_low Color lowest value heatmaps (string). colours_heatmap_high Color highest value heatmaps (string). transform_heatmap Transformation apply visualizing heatmap values ("identity", "log10", "log2"). fontsize_values_heatmap Font size heatmap values (number). show_values_heatmap Display numerical values heatmap tiles (flag). colours_values_heatmap Color heatmap values (string). vertical_spacing Space data row points (number). numeric_plot_type Type visualization numeric data: "bar" "heatmap". y_axis_position Position y-axis ("left" "right"). width controls much space present bars tiles within plot. Can 0-1 values 1 makes bars/tiles take 100% available space (gaps bars). relative_height_numeric many times taller numeric plots relative categorical tile plots. taken account numeric_plot_type == "bar" (number) cli_header Text used h1 header. Included can tweaked packages use ggstack, can customise info messages appear. inter_plot_spacing vertical space add plots. Measured pts (numeric) expand_x vector range expansion constants used add padding around data ensure placed distance away axes. Use convenience function ggplot2::expansion() generate values expand argument. defaults expand scale 0.6 units side. interactive_svg_width, interactive_svg_height width height interactive graphic region (inches). used interactive = TRUE. fontsize_barplot_y_numbers fontsize text describing numeric barplot max & min values (number). max_digits_barplot_y_numbers Number digits round numeric barplot max min values (number). fontsize_y_title Font size y axis titles (.k.data.frame column names) (number). fontface_y_title Font face y axis titles (.k.data.frame column names). One "plain", "italic", "bold", "bold.italic". beautify_text Beautify y-axis text legend titles  human-readable forms (e.g. converting 'my_title' 'Title') (flag). beautify_values Beautify legend values human-readable forms (e.g. converting 'my_value' 'Value') (flag) beautify_function function takes string returns nicely formatted string. Use beautify axis & legend titles beautify_text=TRUE, legend values beautify_values=TRUE. margin_y_title Margin y axis titles discrete properties (numeric properties numeric_plot_type = "heatmap"). Expects NULL, call ggplot2::margin(). margin_y_numbers Margin y axis titles numbers title numeric properties (numeric_plot_type = "bar"). Expects NULL, call ggplot2::margin().

title: Compute Mutual Information — mutinfo
text: Computes mutual information feature features data frame target vector. features discretized using "equalfreq" method infotheo::discretize().

title: Compute Mutual Information — mutinfo
code: mutinfo(features, target, return_colnames = FALSE)

title: Compute Mutual Information — mutinfo
text: features data frame features. discretized using "equalfreq" method (see infotheo::discretize()). target vector (character factor) representing variable compute mutual information . return_colnames Logical; TRUE, returns column names features ordered mutual information target (highest lowest). FALSE, returns mutual information values. (default: FALSE)

title: Compute Mutual Information — mutinfo
text: return_colnames = FALSE, named numeric vector mutual information scores returned (one column features), sorted descending order. names vector correspond column names features. return_colnames = TRUE, ordered column names features returned.

title: Compute Mutual Information — mutinfo
code: data(iris) # Compute mutual information scores mutinfo(iris[1:4], iris[[5]]) #>  Petal.Width Petal.Length Sepal.Length  Sepal.Width  #>    0.8328724    0.8278344    0.4292148    0.2499608   # Get column names ordered by mutual information with target column (most mutual info first) mutinfo(iris[1:4], iris[[5]], return_colnames = TRUE) #> [1] "Petal.Width"  "Petal.Length" "Sepal.Length" "Sepal.Width"

title: ggEDA 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-09 Improved handling infinite values: Infinite values now shown bar plots vertical arrows (/depending whether positive negative). size set na_marker_size size match ‘!’ character used missing data. heatmap visualisations, tile colour infinite values clamped min/max using scales::oob_squish_infinite. Arrow markers optionally added show_na_marker_heatmap = TRUE line_width ggparallel now defaults 0.5 instead NULL. cols_to_plot now dictates order stacking unless col_sort argument supplied order_matches_sort=TRUE. Added beautify_values beautify_function argument ggparallel_options() provide control text beautified (made human readable), reformatted. Added beautify_values beautify_function argument ggstack_options() provide control text beautified (made human readable), reformatted.
